questions: What is the correct implementation sequence for fixing resolveTicket()?
How should the plan handle testability without external mocking libraries?
What verification checks are needed to confirm the fix is complete and regression-free?

answers.answer: The implementation has 4 sequential steps: (1) Add optional fetchFn parameter for DI testability, (2) Implement archived ticket fetch fallback with active-first priority, combined ambiguity detection, and archived-fetch error handling, (3) Add resolveTicket() unit tests covering all acceptance criteria, (4) Run all quality gates to confirm regression-free. Steps 1-2 modify only src/lib/resolve-ticket.ts. Step 3 modifies only src/lib/resolve-ticket.test.ts. Step 4 is verification only.
evidence: tech-research/tech-research.md — Sequential/lazy fetch chosen; DI via optional param; combined array for ambiguity
diagnosis/diagnosis-statement.md — Fix localized to resolveTicket() and its test file; no caller changes needed
src/lib/resolve-ticket.ts:83-127 — Current resolveTicket() implementation; lines 89, 98-99, 102-121 are the three areas to modify

answers.answer: Add an optional third parameter options?: { fetchFn?: typeof hxFetch } to resolveTicket(). Tests create a mock.fn() from node:test that inspects queryParams to distinguish active vs archived API calls and return appropriate test data. This is backward-compatible (all 9 callers pass only 2 args), requires no external dependencies, and was validated by tech-research confirming mock.module is unavailable in the runtime.
evidence: tech-research/tech-research.md — 'mock.module returned undefined in Node 24.14.1'; DI via optional param chosen
tech-research/apl.json — Confirmed DI pattern, mock.fn availability, no external mocking library
src/lib/resolve-ticket.test.ts:1 — Existing tests use node:test describe/it/beforeEach/afterEach pattern

answers.answer: 7 required checks: (1) TypeScript typecheck passes, (2) Full build succeeds, (3) Existing tests pass unchanged, (4) New resolveTicket tests pass covering 9 scenarios (archived by ID/shortId/number, active priority, missing ticket, cross-set ambiguity, archived-only regression, archived fetch error, active fetch error), (5) Only 2 files modified, (6) resolveTicket signature is backward-compatible, (7) Archived fetch error handling does not produce 'not found'.
evidence: ticket.md acceptance criteria #1-#10 — Maps to test cases and verification checks
package.json:16-18 — build: tsc, typecheck: tsc --noEmit, test: tsc && node --test
diagnosis/diagnosis-statement.md — Files to Change: resolve-ticket.ts and resolve-ticket.test.ts only

statement: The implementation plan has 4 atomic steps modifying only 2 files (src/lib/resolve-ticket.ts and src/lib/resolve-ticket.test.ts). Step 1 adds an optional fetchFn parameter for testability. Step 2 implements the core fix: sequential active-first fetch, archived fallback, combined ambiguity detection, and resolution-stage error handling. Step 3 adds 9 test cases covering all acceptance criteria. Step 4 verifies quality gates. The verification plan has 7 required checks covering typecheck, build, tests, file scope, backward compatibility, and error handling.
